title: 10 best herbal teas for fever blister on lip
intro_desc: Herbal teas for fever blister on lip are natural remedies made from herbs that can help reduce the discomfort and healing time of fever blisters. These teas typically contain ingredients like chamomile, lemon balm, and licorice root, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help soothe the pain and reduce the size of the blister. By drinking these teas or using them as a compress, individuals can find relief from the symptoms of fever blisters and reduce the risk of scarring. For example, a person who frequently gets fever blisters may find that regularly drinking chamomile tea helps to reduce the frequency and severity of their outbreaks, improving their quality of life and confidence.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with fever blister on lip because it contains antiviral properties that can help reduce the severity and duration of fever blisters. The herb's active compounds, including rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus, which is responsible for fever blisters. Additionally, lemon balm has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help soothe the pain and discomfort associated with fever blisters. Drinking lemon balm tea or applying it topically to the affected area may provide relief and promote healing.
remedy_properties: Antiviral property: Lemon balm tea contains active constituents like rosmarinic acid and flavonoids that inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus, which causes fever blisters on the lip.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory effects of lemon balm tea, attributed to its rosmarinic acid content, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with fever blisters.
Antioxidant property: Lemon balm tea's high antioxidant content, primarily due to its polyphenol and flavonoid compounds, aids in healing and preventing further damage to the affected lip area.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial properties of lemon balm tea, owing to its eugenol and tannin content, protect the fever blister from secondary bacterial infections and promote faster recovery.
Analgesic property: Lemon balm tea's analgesic effects, resulting from its rosmarinic acid and eugenol content, help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by fever blisters on the lip.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, place one tablespoon of dried lemon balm leaves.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the lemon balm leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Sip the tea slowly, holding it in your mouth near the fever blister for several seconds before swallowing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used to make medicinal teas for fever blisters on the lip due to their antiviral properties, which can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sores.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful for soothing and healing fever blisters.

Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or flowers, the stems of lemon balm still contain beneficial compounds that can help support the healing process of fever blisters when used in medicinal teas.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with fever blister on lip because it contains antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the pain and swelling associated with fever blisters. The tea's antiviral properties can also help to speed up the healing process by inhibiting the spread of the virus that causes fever blisters. Additionally, sage tea has astringent properties that can help to dry out the blister and promote the formation of a scab, which can help to protect the area from infection. Drinking sage tea or applying it directly to the affected area can provide relief from the symptoms of fever blisters and promote healing.
remedy_properties: Antiviral property: Sage tea has antiviral properties that can help reduce the severity of fever blisters on the lip by inhibiting the replication of the herpes simplex virus, the virus that causes fever blisters. The active constituents responsible for sage tea's antiviral properties include rosmarinic acid, carnosol, and carnosic acid.

Anti-inflammatory property: Sage tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the swelling and redness associated with fever blisters on the lip. The active constituents responsible for sage tea's anti-inflammatory properties include rosmarinic acid, ursolic acid, and oleanolic acid.

Analgesic property: Sage tea has analgesic properties that can help relieve the pain associated with fever blisters on the lip. The active constituents responsible for sage tea's analgesic properties include cineole, borneol, and camphor.

Antioxidant property: Sage tea has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin on the lip from further damage caused by fever blisters. The active constituents responsible for sage tea's antioxidant properties include rosmarinic acid, carnosol, and carnosic acid.

Antimicrobial property: Sage tea has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent the development of secondary bacterial infections caused by fever blisters on the lip. The active constituents responsible for sage tea's antimicrobial properties include thujone, 1,8-cineole, and camphor.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried sage leaves to the boiling water.
Allow the sage leaves to steep in the hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the sage leaves from the tea.
Drink the sage tea while it is still warm, up to 3 times a day.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antiviral and anti-inflammatory compounds, sage leaves can help reduce pain and promote healing of fever blisters on the lip.
Flowers: Containing antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, sage flowers can help soothe irritation and protect the affected area from infection.
Buds: High in essential oils, sage buds can provide relief from symptoms of fever blisters, such as itching and burning.
Bark: With astringent properties, sage bark can help dry out the blister and speed up the healing process.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with fever blister on lip because it contains menthol, a natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic compound. Menthol can help reduce pain, swelling, and inflammation associated with fever blisters. Additionally, peppermint tea has antiviral and antimicrobial properties that can help prevent the fever blister from getting worse. The tea's soothing warmth can also provide comfort and relief to the affected area. Drinking peppermint tea may also boost the immune system, helping the body fight off the virus that causes fever blisters. However, it is important to note that while peppermint tea can help alleviate symptoms, it is not a cure for fever blisters.
remedy_properties: Antiviral property: Peppermint tea contains menthol and other compounds that can help inhibit the spread and replication of the virus causing fever blisters, providing relief and reducing the severity of the outbreak.

Anti-inflammatory property: The tea's active ingredients, including menthol, rosmarinic acid, and flavonoids, can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with fever blisters, promoting faster healing.
Analgesic property: Peppermint tea's menthol content can provide a cooling and soothing sensation, acting as a local anesthetic and alleviating pain and discomfort caused by fever blisters.

Antioxidant property: The tea's high levels of antioxidants, such as rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids, can help protect the skin from damage and promote tissue repair, aiding in the healing process of fever blisters.

Antibacterial property: Peppermint tea's antibacterial properties, attributed to menthol and other compounds, can help prevent secondary infections and maintain a clean environment for the fever blister, thus reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, pour 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil.
Crush 1 tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves and add them to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a warm temperature before applying it to the fever blister with a cotton ball.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the peppermint plant are commonly used to make medicinal teas for fever blisters on the lip due to their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers of the peppermint plant contain flavonoids and volatile oils that have been shown to have antiviral and analgesic effects, making them useful for treating fever blisters.

Seeds: Peppermint seeds are rich in essential oils, including menthol, which has been shown to have antiviral and analgesic properties, making them a good choice for medicinal teas for fever blisters on the lip.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with fever blister on lip because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-viral and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce the pain, swelling, and redness associated with fever blisters. Additionally, glycyrrhizin can help boost the immune system and promote healing, which can help the fever blister to resolve more quickly. The tea can also provide soothing relief to the irritated skin around the blister. However, it is important to note that licorice tea should not be applied directly to the blister as it can cause further irritation. It is recommended to drink the tea or use a licensed product that contains licorice extract.

Demulcent: Licorice tea's demulcent property, owing to its mucilage content, forms a protective layer over the affected area, providing soothing relief and reducing irritation caused by fever blisters on the lip.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove the licorice root pieces.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature.
Soak a cotton ball in the tea and apply it to the fever blister for 10-15 minutes, 2-3 times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tea helps with fever blister on lip because it contains several beneficial compounds, such as allantoin, rosmarinic acid, and tannins.
